title: pleurospermum pumilum
intro: Pleurospermum pumilum, also known as small hemlock or small parsley fern, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Pleurospermum pumilum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although proper dosage and preparation should be consulted with a medical professional.
